name: Gibbs Free Energy & Spontaneity
description: Determine reaction spontaneity by combining enthalpy and entropy into the Gibbs free energy change, classifying the sign of ΔG and relating ΔG° to the equilibrium constant.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Define system + reaction"]
    N2["Tabulate ΔH (enthalpy)"]
    N3["Tabulate ΔS (entropy)"]
    N4["ΔG = ΔH − TΔS"]
    N5{"Sign of ΔG?"}
    N6["ΔG < 0: spontaneous"]
    N7["ΔG > 0: non-spontaneous"]
    N8["ΔG = 0: at equilibrium"]
    N9["ΔG° = −RT ln K"]

    N1 --> N2
    N1 --> N3
    N2 --> N4
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|< 0| N6
    N5 -->|> 0| N7
    N5 -->|= 0| N8
    N8 --> N9

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

sources.title: Physical Chemistry, 11th ed. — The Second and Third Laws (Gibbs energy)
sources.authors: Atkins, P.; de Paula, J.; Keeler, J.

sources.title: NIST Chemistry WebBook — thermochemical data
sources.authors: Linstrom, P. J.; Mallard, W. G. (eds.)

nodeDetails.label: Define system + reaction
nodeDetails.detail: Specify reactants, products, T and P

nodeDetails.label: ΔG° = −RT ln K
nodeDetails.detail: Link standard ΔG to equilibrium constant
